heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Hal Ashby. Written by Colin Higgins.
clean: Directed by Hal Ashby. Written by Colin Higgins.

heading: Maude
raw: Dreyfus once wrote from Devil's Island that he would see the most glorious birds. Many years later in Brittany, he realized they had only been sea gulls... For me, they will always be... glorious birds.
clean: Dreyfus once wrote from Devil's Island that he would see the most glorious birds. Many years later in Brittany, he realized they had only been sea gulls... For me, they will always be... glorious birds.

heading: Harold
raw: I haven't lived... I've died a few times.
clean: I haven't lived... I've died a few times.

heading: Priest
raw: I would be remiss in my duty, if I did not tell you, that the idea of...... intercourse... and the fact of your firm, young... body..... commingling, with the... withered flesh... sagging... breasts... and flabby b-... buttocks... makes me want... to vomit.
clean: I would be remiss in my duty, if I did not tell you, that the idea of...... intercourse... and the fact of your firm, young... body..... commingling, with the... withered flesh... sagging... breasts... and flabby b-... buttocks... makes me want... to vomit.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: You hop in any car you want and just drive off?
clean: You hop in any car you want and just drive off?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Well, not any car. I like to keep a variety. I'm always looking for the new experience.
clean: Well, not any car. I like to keep a variety. I'm always looking for the new experience.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: Maybe. Nevertheless, I think you're upsetting people. I don't know if that's right.
clean: Maybe. Nevertheless, I think you're upsetting people. I don't know if that's right.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Well, if some people get upset because they feel they have a hold on some things, I'm merely acting as a gentle reminder: here today, gone tomorrow, so don't get attached to things. Now, with that in mind, I'm not against collecting stuff...
clean: Well, if some people get upset because they feel they have a hold on some things, I'm merely acting as a gentle reminder: here today, gone tomorrow, so don't get attached to things. Now, with that in mind, I'm not against collecting stuff...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: That little tree — it's in trouble. Come on. [Harold and Maude walk over to a tree growing through the sidewalk in front of a building.] Look at it. Oh, it's suffocating. Well, it's the smog. You know, people can live with it, but trees — it gives them asthma. They can't breathe. The leaves, look, they’re turning all brown. Harold, we have got to do something about this life.
clean: That little tree - it's in trouble. Come on. Look at it. Oh, it's suffocating. Well, it's the smog. You know, people can live with it, but trees - it gives them asthma. They can't breathe. The leaves, look, they're turning all brown. Harold, we have got to do something about this life.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: What?
clean: What?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: We'll transplant it. To the forest.
clean: We'll transplant it. To the forest.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: You can't do that
clean: You can't do that

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Why not?
clean: Why not?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: This is public property.
clean: This is public property.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Well, exactly.
clean: Well, exactly.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: Maude?
clean: Maude?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: Do you pray?
clean: Do you pray?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Pray? No. I communicate.
clean: Pray? No. I communicate.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: With God?
clean: With God?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: With Life.
clean: With Life.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Psychiatrist: Tell me, Harold, how many of these, uh, suicides have you performed?
clean: Tell me, Harold, how many of these, uh, suicides have you performed?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: An accurate number would be difficult to gauge.
clean: An accurate number would be difficult to gauge.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Psychiatrist: Well, just give me a rough estimate.
clean: Well, just give me a rough estimate.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: A rough estimate? I'd say... fifteen.
clean: A rough estimate? I'd say... fifteen.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Psychiatrist: Fifteen?
clean: Fifteen?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: That’s a rough estimate.
clean: That's a rough estimate.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Psychiatrist: Were they all done for your mother's benefit?
clean: Were they all done for your mother's benefit?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: No. No, I would not say "benefit."
clean: No. No, I would not say "benefit."

heading: Dialogue
raw: Motorcycle Officer: License, lady?
clean: License, lady?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: I don't have one. I don't believe in them.
clean: I don't have one. I don't believe in them.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Motorcycle Officer: How long you been driving, lady?
clean: How long you been driving, lady?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: About 45 minutes, wouldn't you say, Harold? We were hoping to start sooner; but, you see, it's rather hard to find a truck.
clean: About 45 minutes, wouldn't you say, Harold? We were hoping to start sooner; but, you see, it's rather hard to find a truck.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Motorcycle Officer: This your truck?
clean: This your truck?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Oh, no, I just took it.
clean: Oh, no, I just took it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Harold: You sure have a way with people.
clean: You sure have a way with people.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Maude: Well, they're my species!
clean: Well, they're my species!
